text.title: kanban-scaled-agile-ceremonies Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for kanban-scaled-agile-ceremonies. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Enforce WIP limits as a first-class column setting with a hard block (Jira Premium / Linear Cycles); soft warnings are ignored under pressure.
rule.rationale: WIP limits without enforcement become aspirational. Teams blow past them under pressure without visible feedback; hard blocks force the conversation about priority.

rule.statement: Throughput + 85th-percentile cycle time beats velocity for forecasting; use Monte Carlo simulation on these metrics for date predictions.
rule.rationale: Velocity is a Scrum artifact dependent on sprint length and planning accuracy. Kanban teams deliver on a sliding window; cycle-time percentiles are more stable and actionable for forecasting.

rule.statement: Lock the PI Planning agenda exactly the day before; teams that "tweak" it lose 30+ minutes to confusion.
rule.rationale: PI Planning thrives on predictability. Late agenda changes ripple through breakout groups and reduce decision quality.

rule.statement: Track I&A actions in the same backlog as features with owners and due dates; otherwise they get prioritized to zero and re-surface every PI.
rule.rationale: Improvements without tracking disappear into noise. Make them first-class work items.

rule.statement: Kanban metrics (cycle time, throughput) need ≥6 weeks of clean data before they are trustworthy for forecasting.
rule.rationale: Short windows capture noise (one slow item, holiday week). Six-week minimum smooths anomalies and reveals true process stability.
